entries.title: Statistically impossible yet it happened: The Titanic Omen
stories: In 1898 Morgan Robertson wrote 'Futility' about an unsinkable ship called the Titan that hit an iceberg and sank. 14 years later the Titanic eerily similar in size and design sank exactly the same way.
In 2009 Bulgarian lottery officials drew winning numbers 4 15 23 24 35 42. Four days later the exact same numbers came up again. The odds were 1 in 4 million. No fraud was found.
In 1975 two men named Robert Smith from different states sat next to each other at a Chicago convention. Both worked for the same company in different offices. Both had the same birthday.

entries.title: Statistically impossible yet it happened: Mark Twain's Comet
stories: Mark Twain was born on November 30, 1835 the same day Halley's Comet appeared. He said 'I came in with Halley's Comet and I expect to go out with it.' He died on April 21, 1910 the day after Halley's Comet returned.
In 2009 Bulgarian lottery officials drew winning numbers 4 15 23 24 35 42. Four days later the exact same numbers came up again. The odds were 1 in 4 million. No fraud was found.

In 1972 a man switched hotel rooms because of a bad feeling. His original room was destroyed in a fire. 23 years later he checked into the same hotel and was given the same room he had switched to not knowing.

entries.title: The universe has a weird sense of humor: Death Bed Reunion
entries.hook: The universe has a weird sense of humor:
stories: In 1883 a dying man in Detroit asked for his estranged brother. A stranger arrived hours later who had traveled 200 miles. He had received the same telegram the dying man's family sent because he had the same dying wish at the same time.

In 1937 an American salesman in Paris bought a postcard of the Tomb of the Unknown Soldier. He saw his own father in the crowd photo who had visited Paris 20 years earlier on a trip the son never knew about.
A watch recovered from a Titanic victim stopped at 2:28 AM the exact time the ship sank. In 2012 the watch sold for $28,000 exactly 100 years to the day after the sinking.

entries.title: This is 100% true: Lincoln & Kennedy
stories: Abraham Lincoln was elected to Congress in 1846, John F. Kennedy in 1946. Lincoln was elected President in 1860, Kennedy in 1960. Both were assassinated on a Friday in front of their wives. Both successors were named Johnson.

In the 1920s author Anne Parrish found a book she remembered from childhood in a Paris bookstore. She bought it and showed her husband. When she opened it she found her own name and childhood address written inside as the previous owner.

In 1883 a dying man in Detroit asked for his estranged brother. A stranger arrived hours later who had traveled 200 miles. He had received the same telegram the dying man's family sent because he had the same dying wish at the same time.

entries.title: What are the odds? Prison Photo Coincidence
stories: In 2011 a British man was arrested in Spain. His mugshot turned out identical to a photo taken at the same police station 12 years earlier of a man who committed the exact same crime.
In 2009 Bulgarian lottery officials drew winning numbers 4 15 23 24 35 42. Four days later the exact same numbers came up again. The odds were 1 in 4 million. No fraud was found.
A watch recovered from a Titanic victim stopped at 2:28 AM the exact time the ship sank. In 2012 the watch sold for $28,000 exactly 100 years to the day after the sinking.

entries.title: This is 100% true: Prison Photo Coincidence
stories: In 2011 a British man was arrested in Spain. His mugshot turned out identical to a photo taken at the same police station 12 years earlier of a man who committed the exact same crime.

In 1898 Morgan Robertson wrote 'Futility' about an unsinkable ship called the Titan that hit an iceberg and sank. 14 years later the Titanic eerily similar in size and design sank exactly the same way.

In 1937 an American salesman in Paris bought a postcard of the Tomb of the Unknown Soldier. He saw his own father in the crowd photo who had visited Paris 20 years earlier on a trip the son never knew about.

entries.title: What are the odds? The $50 Bill
stories: In 2002 a woman in Michigan found a $50 bill and deposited it. Months later her mother gave her a birthday card with a $50 bill inside with the exact same serial number as the one she found.
A woman lost her wedding ring swimming off Sweden in 1995. Six years later a fisherman caught a fish and found the ring inside it. The inscription was still readable and he tracked her down.

In 1972 a man switched hotel rooms because of a bad feeling. His original room was destroyed in a fire. 23 years later he checked into the same hotel and was given the same room he had switched to not knowing.

entries.title: What are the odds? The Titanic Omen

In 1972 a man switched hotel rooms because of a bad feeling. His original room was destroyed in a fire. 23 years later he checked into the same hotel and was given the same room he had switched to not knowing.
A watch recovered from a Titanic victim stopped at 2:28 AM the exact time the ship sank. In 2012 the watch sold for $28,000 exactly 100 years to the day after the sinking.

entries.title: You won't believe this coincidence: Mark Twain's Comet
stories: Mark Twain was born on November 30, 1835 the same day Halley's Comet appeared. He said 'I came in with Halley's Comet and I expect to go out with it.' He died on April 21, 1910 the day after Halley's Comet returned.

In the 1920s author Anne Parrish found a book she remembered from childhood in a Paris bookstore. She bought it and showed her husband. When she opened it she found her own name and childhood address written inside as the previous owner.

She bought it and showed her husband. When she opened it she found her own name and childhood address written inside as the previous owner. The Lost Ring. A woman lost her wedding ring swimming off Sweden in 1995. Six years later a fisherman caught a fish and found the ring inside it. The inscription was still readable and he tracked her down.

entries.title: You won't believe this coincidence: The 1919 Molasses Flood
stories: In 1919 a molasses tank exploded in Boston sending a 15-foot wave through the streets killing 21. Exactly 50 years later in 1969 a molasses tank explosion killed 9 in Brooklyn New York.
A woman lost her wedding ring swimming off Sweden in 1995. Six years later a fisherman caught a fish and found the ring inside it. The inscription was still readable and he tracked her down.

In 1937 an American salesman in Paris bought a postcard of the Tomb of the Unknown Soldier. He saw his own father in the crowd photo who had visited Paris 20 years earlier on a trip the son never knew about.

entries.title: What are the odds? The James Twins

In 1736 composer Giuseppe Tartini dreamed the devil played a violin sonata. He wrote down 'The Devil's Trill Sonata' one of the most famous violin works. In 1959 another composer had the same dream and wrote a strikingly similar piece.

entries.title: This is 100% true: The $50 Bill
stories: In 2002 a woman in Michigan found a $50 bill and deposited it. Months later her mother gave her a birthday card with a $50 bill inside with the exact same serial number as the one she found.
In 2011 a British man was arrested in Spain. His mugshot turned out identical to a photo taken at the same police station 12 years earlier of a man who committed the exact same crime.

In 1937 an American salesman in Paris bought a postcard of the Tomb of the Unknown Soldier. He saw his own father in the crowd photo who had visited Paris 20 years earlier on a trip the son never knew about.

entries.title: This is 100% true: The Lottery Replay
stories: In 2009 Bulgarian lottery officials drew winning numbers 4 15 23 24 35 42. Four days later the exact same numbers came up again. The odds were 1 in 4 million. No fraud was found.

In the 1930s Joseph Figlock was walking in Detroit when a baby fell from a window and landed on him. Both survived. A year later the exact same baby fell from a window again and landed on Joseph Figlock again.

Three men in the Canary Islands discovered they were identical triplets separated at birth by an adoption agency as part of a secret study. All three grew up with similar habits and married women with the same name.

In the 1930s Joseph Figlock was walking in Detroit when a baby fell from a window and landed on him. Both survived. A year later the exact same baby fell from a window again and landed on Joseph Figlock again.

In 1972 a man switched hotel rooms because of a bad feeling. His original room was destroyed in a fire. 23 years later he checked into the same hotel and was given the same room he had switched to not knowing.

entries.title: You won't believe this coincidence: Lincoln & Kennedy
stories: Abraham Lincoln was elected to Congress in 1846, John F. Kennedy in 1946. Lincoln was elected President in 1860, Kennedy in 1960. Both were assassinated on a Friday in front of their wives. Both successors were named Johnson.
In 1919 a molasses tank exploded in Boston sending a 15-foot wave through the streets killing 21. Exactly 50 years later in 1969 a molasses tank explosion killed 9 in Brooklyn New York.

Three men in the Canary Islands discovered they were identical triplets separated at birth by an adoption agency as part of a secret study. All three grew up with similar habits and married women with the same name.

entries.title: The universe has a weird sense of humor: The James Twins
entries.hook: The universe has a weird sense of humor:
stories: Two twin boys in Ohio were separated at birth and adopted by different families. Both were named James. Both grew up to be police officers. Both married women named Linda. Both had sons named James Alan and James Allan. Both owned dogs named Toy.

Mark Twain was born on November 30, 1835 the same day Halley's Comet appeared. He said 'I came in with Halley's Comet and I expect to go out with it.' He died on April 21, 1910 the day after Halley's Comet returned.

In 1883 a dying man in Detroit asked for his estranged brother. A stranger arrived hours later who had traveled 200 miles. He had received the same telegram the dying man's family sent because he had the same dying wish at the same time.

entries.title: You won't believe this coincidence: The 1919 Molasses Flood
stories: In 1919 a molasses tank exploded in Boston sending a 15-foot wave through the streets killing 21. Exactly 50 years later in 1969 a molasses tank explosion killed 9 in Brooklyn New York.

In 1937 an American salesman in Paris bought a postcard of the Tomb of the Unknown Soldier. He saw his own father in the crowd photo who had visited Paris 20 years earlier on a trip the son never knew about.

In 1736 composer Giuseppe Tartini dreamed the devil played a violin sonata. He wrote down 'The Devil's Trill Sonata' one of the most famous violin works. In 1959 another composer had the same dream and wrote a strikingly similar piece.

entries.title: Statistically impossible yet it happened: Prison Photo Coincidence
stories: In 2011 a British man was arrested in Spain. His mugshot turned out identical to a photo taken at the same police station 12 years earlier of a man who committed the exact same crime.

In 1898 Morgan Robertson wrote 'Futility' about an unsinkable ship called the Titan that hit an iceberg and sank. 14 years later the Titanic eerily similar in size and design sank exactly the same way.

In the 1930s Joseph Figlock was walking in Detroit when a baby fell from a window and landed on him. Both survived. A year later the exact same baby fell from a window again and landed on Joseph Figlock again.

entries.title: Statistically impossible yet it happened: The $50 Bill
stories: In 2002 a woman in Michigan found a $50 bill and deposited it. Months later her mother gave her a birthday card with a $50 bill inside with the exact same serial number as the one she found.
In 2011 a British man was arrested in Spain. His mugshot turned out identical to a photo taken at the same police station 12 years earlier of a man who committed the exact same crime.

entries.title: You won't believe this coincidence: The Two Emilys
stories: In 2008 two British women named Emily Jones booked separate trips to the same Greek resort. They had never met. They checked into the same room and took the exact same photos at the same spots.

In 1937 an American salesman in Paris bought a postcard of the Tomb of the Unknown Soldier. He saw his own father in the crowd photo who had visited Paris 20 years earlier on a trip the son never knew about.
In 2014 twin brothers in Finland were cycling separately when both were hit by cars on the same road one hour apart. Both survived. The drivers were also twins.

In 1937 an American salesman in Paris bought a postcard of the Tomb of the Unknown Soldier. He saw his own father in the crowd photo who had visited Paris 20 years earlier on a trip the son never knew about.

entries.title: This is 100% true: Lincoln & Kennedy
stories: Abraham Lincoln was elected to Congress in 1846, John F. Kennedy in 1946. Lincoln was elected President in 1860, Kennedy in 1960. Both were assassinated on a Friday in front of their wives. Both successors were named Johnson.

In 1883 a dying man in Detroit asked for his estranged brother. A stranger arrived hours later who had traveled 200 miles. He had received the same telegram the dying man's family sent because he had the same dying wish at the same time.

In 1736 composer Giuseppe Tartini dreamed the devil played a violin sonata. He wrote down 'The Devil's Trill Sonata' one of the most famous violin works. In 1959 another composer had the same dream and wrote a strikingly similar piece.

entries.title: Statistically impossible yet it happened: Falling Baby
stories: In the 1930s Joseph Figlock was walking in Detroit when a baby fell from a window and landed on him. Both survived. A year later the exact same baby fell from a window again and landed on Joseph Figlock again.

In 1937 an American salesman in Paris bought a postcard of the Tomb of the Unknown Soldier. He saw his own father in the crowd photo who had visited Paris 20 years earlier on a trip the son never knew about.

In 1972 a man switched hotel rooms because of a bad feeling. His original room was destroyed in a fire. 23 years later he checked into the same hotel and was given the same room he had switched to not knowing.

entries.title: The universe has a weird sense of humor: The Bookstore Dream
entries.hook: The universe has a weird sense of humor:
stories: In the 1920s author Anne Parrish found a book she remembered from childhood in a Paris bookstore. She bought it and showed her husband. When she opened it she found her own name and childhood address written inside as the previous owner.
In 2008 two British women named Emily Jones booked separate trips to the same Greek resort. They had never met. They checked into the same room and took the exact same photos at the same spots.
A woman lost her wedding ring swimming off Sweden in 1995. Six years later a fisherman caught a fish and found the ring inside it. The inscription was still readable and he tracked her down.

entries.title: You won't believe this coincidence: The Titanic Omen
stories: In 1898 Morgan Robertson wrote 'Futility' about an unsinkable ship called the Titan that hit an iceberg and sank. 14 years later the Titanic eerily similar in size and design sank exactly the same way.

Three men in the Canary Islands discovered they were identical triplets separated at birth by an adoption agency as part of a secret study. All three grew up with similar habits and married women with the same name.

In 1972 a man switched hotel rooms because of a bad feeling. His original room was destroyed in a fire. 23 years later he checked into the same hotel and was given the same room he had switched to not knowing.

In 1959 another composer had the same dream and wrote a strikingly similar piece. The Hotel Fire. In 1972 a man switched hotel rooms because of a bad feeling. His original room was destroyed in a fire. 23 years later he checked into the same hotel and was given the same room he had switched to not knowing. The Watch That Stopped. A watch recovered from a Titanic victim stopped at 2:28 AM the exact time the ship sank. In 2012 the watch sold for $28,000 exactly 100 years to the day after the sinking.

entries.title: This is 100% true: The $50 Bill
stories: In 2002 a woman in Michigan found a $50 bill and deposited it. Months later her mother gave her a birthday card with a $50 bill inside with the exact same serial number as the one she found.

In the 1930s Joseph Figlock was walking in Detroit when a baby fell from a window and landed on him. Both survived. A year later the exact same baby fell from a window again and landed on Joseph Figlock again.

Three men in the Canary Islands discovered they were identical triplets separated at birth by an adoption agency as part of a secret study. All three grew up with similar habits and married women with the same name.

entries.title: What are the odds? The 1919 Molasses Flood
stories: In 1919 a molasses tank exploded in Boston sending a 15-foot wave through the streets killing 21. Exactly 50 years later in 1969 a molasses tank explosion killed 9 in Brooklyn New York.
In 2009 Bulgarian lottery officials drew winning numbers 4 15 23 24 35 42. Four days later the exact same numbers came up again. The odds were 1 in 4 million. No fraud was found.
In 1975 two men named Robert Smith from different states sat next to each other at a Chicago convention. Both worked for the same company in different offices. Both had the same birthday.

entries.title: The universe has a weird sense of humor: The Bookstore Dream
entries.hook: The universe has a weird sense of humor:
stories: In the 1920s author Anne Parrish found a book she remembered from childhood in a Paris bookstore. She bought it and showed her husband. When she opened it she found her own name and childhood address written inside as the previous owner.

In the 1930s Joseph Figlock was walking in Detroit when a baby fell from a window and landed on him. Both survived. A year later the exact same baby fell from a window again and landed on Joseph Figlock again.
A watch recovered from a Titanic victim stopped at 2:28 AM the exact time the ship sank. In 2012 the watch sold for $28,000 exactly 100 years to the day after the sinking.

entries.title: You won't believe this coincidence: Mark Twain's Comet
stories: Mark Twain was born on November 30, 1835 the same day Halley's Comet appeared. He said 'I came in with Halley's Comet and I expect to go out with it.' He died on April 21, 1910 the day after Halley's Comet returned.
In 1919 a molasses tank exploded in Boston sending a 15-foot wave through the streets killing 21. Exactly 50 years later in 1969 a molasses tank explosion killed 9 in Brooklyn New York.

In 1883 a dying man in Detroit asked for his estranged brother. A stranger arrived hours later who had traveled 200 miles. He had received the same telegram the dying man's family sent because he had the same dying wish at the same time.

entries.title: What are the odds? Lincoln & Kennedy
stories: Abraham Lincoln was elected to Congress in 1846, John F. Kennedy in 1946. Lincoln was elected President in 1860, Kennedy in 1960. Both were assassinated on a Friday in front of their wives. Both successors were named Johnson.
In 2002 a woman in Michigan found a $50 bill and deposited it. Months later her mother gave her a birthday card with a $50 bill inside with the exact same serial number as the one she found.

In 1898 Morgan Robertson wrote 'Futility' about an unsinkable ship called the Titan that hit an iceberg and sank. 14 years later the Titanic eerily similar in size and design sank exactly the same way.

entries.title: Statistically impossible yet it happened: The James Twins
stories: Two twin boys in Ohio were separated at birth and adopted by different families. Both were named James. Both grew up to be police officers. Both married women named Linda. Both had sons named James Alan and James Allan. Both owned dogs named Toy.

Abraham Lincoln was elected to Congress in 1846, John F. Kennedy in 1946. Lincoln was elected President in 1860, Kennedy in 1960. Both were assassinated on a Friday in front of their wives. Both successors were named Johnson.
A woman lost her wedding ring swimming off Sweden in 1995. Six years later a fisherman caught a fish and found the ring inside it. The inscription was still readable and he tracked her down.

entries.title: The universe has a weird sense of humor: Prison Photo Coincidence
entries.hook: The universe has a weird sense of humor:
stories: In 2011 a British man was arrested in Spain. His mugshot turned out identical to a photo taken at the same police station 12 years earlier of a man who committed the exact same crime.

In the 1930s Joseph Figlock was walking in Detroit when a baby fell from a window and landed on him. Both survived. A year later the exact same baby fell from a window again and landed on Joseph Figlock again.
In 2014 twin brothers in Finland were cycling separately when both were hit by cars on the same road one hour apart. Both survived. The drivers were also twins.

entries.title: What are the odds? Mark Twain's Comet
stories: Mark Twain was born on November 30, 1835 the same day Halley's Comet appeared. He said 'I came in with Halley's Comet and I expect to go out with it.' He died on April 21, 1910 the day after Halley's Comet returned.
In 1919 a molasses tank exploded in Boston sending a 15-foot wave through the streets killing 21. Exactly 50 years later in 1969 a molasses tank explosion killed 9 in Brooklyn New York.

In 1972 a man switched hotel rooms because of a bad feeling. His original room was destroyed in a fire. 23 years later he checked into the same hotel and was given the same room he had switched to not knowing.

entries.title: Statistically impossible yet it happened: Death Bed Reunion
stories: In 1883 a dying man in Detroit asked for his estranged brother. A stranger arrived hours later who had traveled 200 miles. He had received the same telegram the dying man's family sent because he had the same dying wish at the same time.

In 1736 composer Giuseppe Tartini dreamed the devil played a violin sonata. He wrote down 'The Devil's Trill Sonata' one of the most famous violin works. In 1959 another composer had the same dream and wrote a strikingly similar piece.
In 2014 twin brothers in Finland were cycling separately when both were hit by cars on the same road one hour apart. Both survived. The drivers were also twins.

entries.title: You won't believe this coincidence: The James Twins
stories: Two twin boys in Ohio were separated at birth and adopted by different families. Both were named James. Both grew up to be police officers. Both married women named Linda. Both had sons named James Alan and James Allan. Both owned dogs named Toy.
In 1919 a molasses tank exploded in Boston sending a 15-foot wave through the streets killing 21. Exactly 50 years later in 1969 a molasses tank explosion killed 9 in Brooklyn New York.
A watch recovered from a Titanic victim stopped at 2:28 AM the exact time the ship sank. In 2012 the watch sold for $28,000 exactly 100 years to the day after the sinking.

entries.script: The Titanic Omen. In 1898 Morgan Robertson wrote 'Futility' about an unsinkable ship called the Titan that hit an iceberg and sank. 14 years later the Titanic eerily similar in size and design sank exactly the same way. The Unknown Soldier. In 1937 an American salesman in Paris bought a postcard of the Tomb of the Unknown Soldier. He saw his own father in the crowd photo who had visited Paris 20 years earlier on a trip the son never knew about. The Hotel Fire. In 1972 a man switched hotel rooms because of a bad feeling. His original room was destroyed in a fire. 23 years later he checked into the same hotel and was given the same room he had switched to not knowing.

entries.title: You won't believe this coincidence: Lincoln & Kennedy
stories: Abraham Lincoln was elected to Congress in 1846, John F. Kennedy in 1946. Lincoln was elected President in 1860, Kennedy in 1960. Both were assassinated on a Friday in front of their wives. Both successors were named Johnson.

In 1883 a dying man in Detroit asked for his estranged brother. A stranger arrived hours later who had traveled 200 miles. He had received the same telegram the dying man's family sent because he had the same dying wish at the same time.
In 1975 two men named Robert Smith from different states sat next to each other at a Chicago convention. Both worked for the same company in different offices. Both had the same birthday.

entries.title: This is 100% true: Lincoln & Kennedy
stories: Abraham Lincoln was elected to Congress in 1846, John F. Kennedy in 1946. Lincoln was elected President in 1860, Kennedy in 1960. Both were assassinated on a Friday in front of their wives. Both successors were named Johnson.
In 1919 a molasses tank exploded in Boston sending a 15-foot wave through the streets killing 21. Exactly 50 years later in 1969 a molasses tank explosion killed 9 in Brooklyn New York.
A watch recovered from a Titanic victim stopped at 2:28 AM the exact time the ship sank. In 2012 the watch sold for $28,000 exactly 100 years to the day after the sinking.

entries.title: The universe has a weird sense of humor: Prison Photo Coincidence
entries.hook: The universe has a weird sense of humor:
stories: In 2011 a British man was arrested in Spain. His mugshot turned out identical to a photo taken at the same police station 12 years earlier of a man who committed the exact same crime.

In 1898 Morgan Robertson wrote 'Futility' about an unsinkable ship called the Titan that hit an iceberg and sank. 14 years later the Titanic eerily similar in size and design sank exactly the same way.
In 2008 two British women named Emily Jones booked separate trips to the same Greek resort. They had never met. They checked into the same room and took the exact same photos at the same spots.

entries.hook: The universe has a weird sense of humor:
stories: A woman lost her wedding ring swimming off Sweden in 1995. Six years later a fisherman caught a fish and found the ring inside it. The inscription was still readable and he tracked her down.
In 1975 two men named Robert Smith from different states sat next to each other at a Chicago convention. Both worked for the same company in different offices. Both had the same birthday.

In 1972 a man switched hotel rooms because of a bad feeling. His original room was destroyed in a fire. 23 years later he checked into the same hotel and was given the same room he had switched to not knowing.

entries.title: Statistically impossible yet it happened: The James Twins
stories: Two twin boys in Ohio were separated at birth and adopted by different families. Both were named James. Both grew up to be police officers. Both married women named Linda. Both had sons named James Alan and James Allan. Both owned dogs named Toy.

In the 1920s author Anne Parrish found a book she remembered from childhood in a Paris bookstore. She bought it and showed her husband. When she opened it she found her own name and childhood address written inside as the previous owner.

In 1972 a man switched hotel rooms because of a bad feeling. His original room was destroyed in a fire. 23 years later he checked into the same hotel and was given the same room he had switched to not knowing.

entries.title: Statistically impossible yet it happened: Lincoln & Kennedy
stories: Abraham Lincoln was elected to Congress in 1846, John F. Kennedy in 1946. Lincoln was elected President in 1860, Kennedy in 1960. Both were assassinated on a Friday in front of their wives. Both successors were named Johnson.
In 2009 Bulgarian lottery officials drew winning numbers 4 15 23 24 35 42. Four days later the exact same numbers came up again. The odds were 1 in 4 million. No fraud was found.

Three men in the Canary Islands discovered they were identical triplets separated at birth by an adoption agency as part of a secret study. All three grew up with similar habits and married women with the same name.

entries.title: This is 100% true: The $50 Bill
stories: In 2002 a woman in Michigan found a $50 bill and deposited it. Months later her mother gave her a birthday card with a $50 bill inside with the exact same serial number as the one she found.

In 1883 a dying man in Detroit asked for his estranged brother. A stranger arrived hours later who had traveled 200 miles. He had received the same telegram the dying man's family sent because he had the same dying wish at the same time.

In the 1930s Joseph Figlock was walking in Detroit when a baby fell from a window and landed on him. Both survived. A year later the exact same baby fell from a window again and landed on Joseph Figlock again.

entries.title: Statistically impossible yet it happened: Mark Twain's Comet
stories: Mark Twain was born on November 30, 1835 the same day Halley's Comet appeared. He said 'I came in with Halley's Comet and I expect to go out with it.' He died on April 21, 1910 the day after Halley's Comet returned.

entries.title: This is 100% true: Death Bed Reunion
stories: In 1883 a dying man in Detroit asked for his estranged brother. A stranger arrived hours later who had traveled 200 miles. He had received the same telegram the dying man's family sent because he had the same dying wish at the same time.

In 1937 an American salesman in Paris bought a postcard of the Tomb of the Unknown Soldier. He saw his own father in the crowd photo who had visited Paris 20 years earlier on a trip the son never knew about.

entries.title: You won't believe this coincidence: The James Twins
stories: Two twin boys in Ohio were separated at birth and adopted by different families. Both were named James. Both grew up to be police officers. Both married women named Linda. Both had sons named James Alan and James Allan. Both owned dogs named Toy.
In 2008 two British women named Emily Jones booked separate trips to the same Greek resort. They had never met. They checked into the same room and took the exact same photos at the same spots.
In 1975 two men named Robert Smith from different states sat next to each other at a Chicago convention. Both worked for the same company in different offices. Both had the same birthday.

entries.title: You won't believe this coincidence: Lincoln & Kennedy

entries.title: You won't believe this coincidence: The Bookstore Dream
stories: In the 1920s author Anne Parrish found a book she remembered from childhood in a Paris bookstore. She bought it and showed her husband. When she opened it she found her own name and childhood address written inside as the previous owner.
In 2011 a British man was arrested in Spain. His mugshot turned out identical to a photo taken at the same police station 12 years earlier of a man who committed the exact same crime.

entries.title: What are the odds? The $50 Bill
stories: In 2002 a woman in Michigan found a $50 bill and deposited it. Months later her mother gave her a birthday card with a $50 bill inside with the exact same serial number as the one she found.
A woman lost her wedding ring swimming off Sweden in 1995. Six years later a fisherman caught a fish and found the ring inside it. The inscription was still readable and he tracked her down.
A watch recovered from a Titanic victim stopped at 2:28 AM the exact time the ship sank. In 2012 the watch sold for $28,000 exactly 100 years to the day after the sinking.

entries.title: You won't believe this coincidence: Mark Twain's Comet
stories: Mark Twain was born on November 30, 1835 the same day Halley's Comet appeared. He said 'I came in with Halley's Comet and I expect to go out with it.' He died on April 21, 1910 the day after Halley's Comet returned.
In 2009 Bulgarian lottery officials drew winning numbers 4 15 23 24 35 42. Four days later the exact same numbers came up again. The odds were 1 in 4 million. No fraud was found.

In 1937 an American salesman in Paris bought a postcard of the Tomb of the Unknown Soldier. He saw his own father in the crowd photo who had visited Paris 20 years earlier on a trip the son never knew about.

In 1919 a molasses tank exploded in Boston sending a 15-foot wave through the streets killing 21. Exactly 50 years later in 1969 a molasses tank explosion killed 9 in Brooklyn New York. The Unknown Soldier. In 1937 an American salesman in Paris bought a postcard of the Tomb of the Unknown Soldier. He saw his own father in the crowd photo who had visited Paris 20 years earlier on a trip the son never knew about.

stories: Mark Twain was born on November 30, 1835 the same day Halley's Comet appeared. He said 'I came in with Halley's Comet and I expect to go out with it.' He died on April 21, 1910 the day after Halley's Comet returned.
In 2009 Bulgarian lottery officials drew winning numbers 4 15 23 24 35 42. Four days later the exact same numbers came up again. The odds were 1 in 4 million. No fraud was found.

In 1883 a dying man in Detroit asked for his estranged brother. A stranger arrived hours later who had traveled 200 miles. He had received the same telegram the dying man's family sent because he had the same dying wish at the same time.

entries.title: The universe has a weird sense of humor: The James Twins
entries.hook: The universe has a weird sense of humor:
stories: Two twin boys in Ohio were separated at birth and adopted by different families. Both were named James. Both grew up to be police officers. Both married women named Linda. Both had sons named James Alan and James Allan. Both owned dogs named Toy.
In 2008 two British women named Emily Jones booked separate trips to the same Greek resort. They had never met. They checked into the same room and took the exact same photos at the same spots.

In 1972 a man switched hotel rooms because of a bad feeling. His original room was destroyed in a fire. 23 years later he checked into the same hotel and was given the same room he had switched to not knowing.

entries.title: You won't believe this coincidence: Prison Photo Coincidence
stories: In 2011 a British man was arrested in Spain. His mugshot turned out identical to a photo taken at the same police station 12 years earlier of a man who committed the exact same crime.

In 1883 a dying man in Detroit asked for his estranged brother. A stranger arrived hours later who had traveled 200 miles. He had received the same telegram the dying man's family sent because he had the same dying wish at the same time.
A woman lost her wedding ring swimming off Sweden in 1995. Six years later a fisherman caught a fish and found the ring inside it. The inscription was still readable and he tracked her down.

entries.title: The universe has a weird sense of humor: Falling Baby
entries.hook: The universe has a weird sense of humor:
stories: In the 1930s Joseph Figlock was walking in Detroit when a baby fell from a window and landed on him. Both survived. A year later the exact same baby fell from a window again and landed on Joseph Figlock again.

In 1937 an American salesman in Paris bought a postcard of the Tomb of the Unknown Soldier. He saw his own father in the crowd photo who had visited Paris 20 years earlier on a trip the son never knew about.

In 1736 composer Giuseppe Tartini dreamed the devil played a violin sonata. He wrote down 'The Devil's Trill Sonata' one of the most famous violin works. In 1959 another composer had the same dream and wrote a strikingly similar piece.

In the 1930s Joseph Figlock was walking in Detroit when a baby fell from a window and landed on him. Both survived. A year later the exact same baby fell from a window again and landed on Joseph Figlock again.

In 1937 an American salesman in Paris bought a postcard of the Tomb of the Unknown Soldier. He saw his own father in the crowd photo who had visited Paris 20 years earlier on a trip the son never knew about.

entries.title: You won't believe this coincidence: Lincoln & Kennedy
stories: Abraham Lincoln was elected to Congress in 1846, John F. Kennedy in 1946. Lincoln was elected President in 1860, Kennedy in 1960. Both were assassinated on a Friday in front of their wives. Both successors were named Johnson.

In the 1930s Joseph Figlock was walking in Detroit when a baby fell from a window and landed on him. Both survived. A year later the exact same baby fell from a window again and landed on Joseph Figlock again.
A watch recovered from a Titanic victim stopped at 2:28 AM the exact time the ship sank. In 2012 the watch sold for $28,000 exactly 100 years to the day after the sinking.

entries.title: This is 100% true: The James Twins
stories: Two twin boys in Ohio were separated at birth and adopted by different families. Both were named James. Both grew up to be police officers. Both married women named Linda. Both had sons named James Alan and James Allan. Both owned dogs named Toy.

In the 1920s author Anne Parrish found a book she remembered from childhood in a Paris bookstore. She bought it and showed her husband. When she opened it she found her own name and childhood address written inside as the previous owner.
In 2002 a woman in Michigan found a $50 bill and deposited it. Months later her mother gave her a birthday card with a $50 bill inside with the exact same serial number as the one she found.

entries.title: The universe has a weird sense of humor: The 1919 Molasses Flood
entries.hook: The universe has a weird sense of humor:
stories: In 1919 a molasses tank exploded in Boston sending a 15-foot wave through the streets killing 21. Exactly 50 years later in 1969 a molasses tank explosion killed 9 in Brooklyn New York.
In 2008 two British women named Emily Jones booked separate trips to the same Greek resort. They had never met. They checked into the same room and took the exact same photos at the same spots.

Three men in the Canary Islands discovered they were identical triplets separated at birth by an adoption agency as part of a secret study. All three grew up with similar habits and married women with the same name.

entries.title: You won't believe this coincidence: The Two Emilys
stories: In 2008 two British women named Emily Jones booked separate trips to the same Greek resort. They had never met. They checked into the same room and took the exact same photos at the same spots.
In 2009 Bulgarian lottery officials drew winning numbers 4 15 23 24 35 42. Four days later the exact same numbers came up again. The odds were 1 in 4 million. No fraud was found.
In 2014 twin brothers in Finland were cycling separately when both were hit by cars on the same road one hour apart. Both survived. The drivers were also twins.

entries.title: Statistically impossible yet it happened: Mark Twain's Comet
stories: Mark Twain was born on November 30, 1835 the same day Halley's Comet appeared. He said 'I came in with Halley's Comet and I expect to go out with it.' He died on April 21, 1910 the day after Halley's Comet returned.
In 2008 two British women named Emily Jones booked separate trips to the same Greek resort. They had never met. They checked into the same room and took the exact same photos at the same spots.

In 1883 a dying man in Detroit asked for his estranged brother. A stranger arrived hours later who had traveled 200 miles. He had received the same telegram the dying man's family sent because he had the same dying wish at the same time.

Months later her mother gave her a birthday card with a $50 bill inside with the exact same serial number as the one she found. The Composer Dream. In 2012 the watch sold for $28,000 exactly 100 years to the day after the sinking.

entries.title: What are the odds? Lincoln & Kennedy
stories: Abraham Lincoln was elected to Congress in 1846, John F. Kennedy in 1946. Lincoln was elected President in 1860, Kennedy in 1960. Both were assassinated on a Friday in front of their wives. Both successors were named Johnson.
In 2008 two British women named Emily Jones booked separate trips to the same Greek resort. They had never met. They checked into the same room and took the exact same photos at the same spots.
In 2009 Bulgarian lottery officials drew winning numbers 4 15 23 24 35 42. Four days later the exact same numbers came up again. The odds were 1 in 4 million. No fraud was found.

entries.title: What are the odds? The James Twins
stories: Two twin boys in Ohio were separated at birth and adopted by different families. Both were named James. Both grew up to be police officers. Both married women named Linda. Both had sons named James Alan and James Allan. Both owned dogs named Toy.
In 1919 a molasses tank exploded in Boston sending a 15-foot wave through the streets killing 21. Exactly 50 years later in 1969 a molasses tank explosion killed 9 in Brooklyn New York.
In 2009 Bulgarian lottery officials drew winning numbers 4 15 23 24 35 42. Four days later the exact same numbers came up again. The odds were 1 in 4 million. No fraud was found.

entries.title: What are the odds? Falling Baby
stories: In the 1930s Joseph Figlock was walking in Detroit when a baby fell from a window and landed on him. Both survived. A year later the exact same baby fell from a window again and landed on Joseph Figlock again.

In 1937 an American salesman in Paris bought a postcard of the Tomb of the Unknown Soldier. He saw his own father in the crowd photo who had visited Paris 20 years earlier on a trip the son never knew about.
A watch recovered from a Titanic victim stopped at 2:28 AM the exact time the ship sank. In 2012 the watch sold for $28,000 exactly 100 years to the day after the sinking.

entries.title: The universe has a weird sense of humor: Lincoln & Kennedy

In 1975 two men named Robert Smith from different states sat next to each other at a Chicago convention. Both worked for the same company in different offices. Both had the same birthday.
In 2014 twin brothers in Finland were cycling separately when both were hit by cars on the same road one hour apart. Both survived. The drivers were also twins.

entries.title: The universe has a weird sense of humor: Mark Twain's Comet
entries.hook: The universe has a weird sense of humor:
stories: Mark Twain was born on November 30, 1835 the same day Halley's Comet appeared. He said 'I came in with Halley's Comet and I expect to go out with it.' He died on April 21, 1910 the day after Halley's Comet returned.
In 2009 Bulgarian lottery officials drew winning numbers 4 15 23 24 35 42. Four days later the exact same numbers came up again. The odds were 1 in 4 million. No fraud was found.
In 2014 twin brothers in Finland were cycling separately when both were hit by cars on the same road one hour apart. Both survived. The drivers were also twins.

entries.title: You won't believe this coincidence: The $50 Bill
stories: In 2002 a woman in Michigan found a $50 bill and deposited it. Months later her mother gave her a birthday card with a $50 bill inside with the exact same serial number as the one she found.
In 2008 two British women named Emily Jones booked separate trips to the same Greek resort. They had never met. They checked into the same room and took the exact same photos at the same spots.

In 1972 a man switched hotel rooms because of a bad feeling. His original room was destroyed in a fire. 23 years later he checked into the same hotel and was given the same room he had switched to not knowing.

entries.title: The universe has a weird sense of humor: Lincoln & Kennedy
entries.hook: The universe has a weird sense of humor:
stories: Abraham Lincoln was elected to Congress in 1846, John F. Kennedy in 1946. Lincoln was elected President in 1860, Kennedy in 1960. Both were assassinated on a Friday in front of their wives. Both successors were named Johnson.

Mark Twain was born on November 30, 1835 the same day Halley's Comet appeared. He said 'I came in with Halley's Comet and I expect to go out with it.' He died on April 21, 1910 the day after Halley's Comet returned.

Three men in the Canary Islands discovered they were identical triplets separated at birth by an adoption agency as part of a secret study. All three grew up with similar habits and married women with the same name.

entries.title: The universe has a weird sense of humor: The Titanic Omen
entries.hook: The universe has a weird sense of humor:
stories: In 1898 Morgan Robertson wrote 'Futility' about an unsinkable ship called the Titan that hit an iceberg and sank. 14 years later the Titanic eerily similar in size and design sank exactly the same way.
In 1975 two men named Robert Smith from different states sat next to each other at a Chicago convention. Both worked for the same company in different offices. Both had the same birthday.
In 2014 twin brothers in Finland were cycling separately when both were hit by cars on the same road one hour apart. Both survived. The drivers were also twins.

entries.title: The universe has a weird sense of humor: The James Twins
entries.hook: The universe has a weird sense of humor:
stories: Two twin boys in Ohio were separated at birth and adopted by different families. Both were named James. Both grew up to be police officers. Both married women named Linda. Both had sons named James Alan and James Allan. Both owned dogs named Toy.

Abraham Lincoln was elected to Congress in 1846, John F. Kennedy in 1946. Lincoln was elected President in 1860, Kennedy in 1960. Both were assassinated on a Friday in front of their wives. Both successors were named Johnson.

Three men in the Canary Islands discovered they were identical triplets separated at birth by an adoption agency as part of a secret study. All three grew up with similar habits and married women with the same name.

entries.title: What are the odds? Death Bed Reunion
stories: In 1883 a dying man in Detroit asked for his estranged brother. A stranger arrived hours later who had traveled 200 miles. He had received the same telegram the dying man's family sent because he had the same dying wish at the same time.

In 1972 a man switched hotel rooms because of a bad feeling. His original room was destroyed in a fire. 23 years later he checked into the same hotel and was given the same room he had switched to not knowing.
A watch recovered from a Titanic victim stopped at 2:28 AM the exact time the ship sank. In 2012 the watch sold for $28,000 exactly 100 years to the day after the sinking.

entries.title: You won't believe this coincidence: Death Bed Reunion
stories: In 1883 a dying man in Detroit asked for his estranged brother. A stranger arrived hours later who had traveled 200 miles. He had received the same telegram the dying man's family sent because he had the same dying wish at the same time.
A woman lost her wedding ring swimming off Sweden in 1995. Six years later a fisherman caught a fish and found the ring inside it. The inscription was still readable and he tracked her down.
In 2014 twin brothers in Finland were cycling separately when both were hit by cars on the same road one hour apart. Both survived. The drivers were also twins.

entries.title: Statistically impossible yet it happened: Lincoln & Kennedy

entries.title: What are the odds? Prison Photo Coincidence
stories: In 2011 a British man was arrested in Spain. His mugshot turned out identical to a photo taken at the same police station 12 years earlier of a man who committed the exact same crime.

In 1898 Morgan Robertson wrote 'Futility' about an unsinkable ship called the Titan that hit an iceberg and sank. 14 years later the Titanic eerily similar in size and design sank exactly the same way.

Three men in the Canary Islands discovered they were identical triplets separated at birth by an adoption agency as part of a secret study. All three grew up with similar habits and married women with the same name.

entries.script: The Lost Ring. A woman lost her wedding ring swimming off Sweden in 1995. Six years later a fisherman caught a fish and found the ring inside it. The inscription was still readable and he tracked her down. The Composer Dream. In 1736 composer Giuseppe Tartini dreamed the devil played a violin sonata. He wrote down 'The Devil's Trill Sonata' one of the most famous violin works. In 1959 another composer had the same dream and wrote a strikingly similar piece. The Watch That Stopped. In 1919 a molasses tank exploded in Boston sending a 15-foot wave through the streets killing 21. Exactly 50 years later in 1969 a molasses tank explosion killed 9 in Brooklyn New York.

entries.title: You won't believe this coincidence: The Bookstore Dream
stories: In the 1920s author Anne Parrish found a book she remembered from childhood in a Paris bookstore. She bought it and showed her husband. When she opened it she found her own name and childhood address written inside as the previous owner.
In 1919 a molasses tank exploded in Boston sending a 15-foot wave through the streets killing 21. Exactly 50 years later in 1969 a molasses tank explosion killed 9 in Brooklyn New York.

In 1883 a dying man in Detroit asked for his estranged brother. A stranger arrived hours later who had traveled 200 miles. He had received the same telegram the dying man's family sent because he had the same dying wish at the same time.

entries.title: The universe has a weird sense of humor: Lincoln & Kennedy
entries.hook: The universe has a weird sense of humor:
stories: Abraham Lincoln was elected to Congress in 1846, John F. Kennedy in 1946. Lincoln was elected President in 1860, Kennedy in 1960. Both were assassinated on a Friday in front of their wives. Both successors were named Johnson.
In 2009 Bulgarian lottery officials drew winning numbers 4 15 23 24 35 42. Four days later the exact same numbers came up again. The odds were 1 in 4 million. No fraud was found.
A watch recovered from a Titanic victim stopped at 2:28 AM the exact time the ship sank. In 2012 the watch sold for $28,000 exactly 100 years to the day after the sinking.

Months later her mother gave her a birthday card with a $50 bill inside with the exact same serial number as the one she found. The Lost Ring. A woman lost her wedding ring swimming off Sweden in 1995. Six years later a fisherman caught a fish and found the ring inside it. The inscription was still readable and he tracked her down.

In 2008 two British women named Emily Jones booked separate trips to the same Greek resort. They had never met. They checked into the same room and took the exact same photos at the same spots.

In 1736 composer Giuseppe Tartini dreamed the devil played a violin sonata. He wrote down 'The Devil's Trill Sonata' one of the most famous violin works. In 1959 another composer had the same dream and wrote a strikingly similar piece.

entries.title: Statistically impossible yet it happened: Prison Photo Coincidence
stories: In 2011 a British man was arrested in Spain. His mugshot turned out identical to a photo taken at the same police station 12 years earlier of a man who committed the exact same crime.

entries.title: You won't believe this coincidence: Lincoln & Kennedy
stories: Abraham Lincoln was elected to Congress in 1846, John F. Kennedy in 1946. Lincoln was elected President in 1860, Kennedy in 1960. Both were assassinated on a Friday in front of their wives. Both successors were named Johnson.

In the 1930s Joseph Figlock was walking in Detroit when a baby fell from a window and landed on him. Both survived. A year later the exact same baby fell from a window again and landed on Joseph Figlock again.
In 2014 twin brothers in Finland were cycling separately when both were hit by cars on the same road one hour apart. Both survived. The drivers were also twins.

entries.title: The universe has a weird sense of humor: The James Twins
entries.hook: The universe has a weird sense of humor:
stories: Two twin boys in Ohio were separated at birth and adopted by different families. Both were named James. Both grew up to be police officers. Both married women named Linda. Both had sons named James Alan and James Allan. Both owned dogs named Toy.

In 1883 a dying man in Detroit asked for his estranged brother. A stranger arrived hours later who had traveled 200 miles. He had received the same telegram the dying man's family sent because he had the same dying wish at the same time.
A woman lost her wedding ring swimming off Sweden in 1995. Six years later a fisherman caught a fish and found the ring inside it. The inscription was still readable and he tracked her down.

entries.title: The universe has a weird sense of humor: Lincoln & Kennedy
entries.hook: The universe has a weird sense of humor:
stories: Abraham Lincoln was elected to Congress in 1846, John F. Kennedy in 1946. Lincoln was elected President in 1860, Kennedy in 1960. Both were assassinated on a Friday in front of their wives. Both successors were named Johnson.

In the 1920s author Anne Parrish found a book she remembered from childhood in a Paris bookstore. She bought it and showed her husband. When she opened it she found her own name and childhood address written inside as the previous owner.

In 1937 an American salesman in Paris bought a postcard of the Tomb of the Unknown Soldier. He saw his own father in the crowd photo who had visited Paris 20 years earlier on a trip the son never knew about.

entries.title: You won't believe this coincidence: The Lost Ring

In 2014 twin brothers in Finland were cycling separately when both were hit by cars on the same road one hour apart. Both survived. The drivers were also twins.

In 1972 a man switched hotel rooms because of a bad feeling. His original room was destroyed in a fire. 23 years later he checked into the same hotel and was given the same room he had switched to not knowing.

entries.title: You won't believe this coincidence: The Titanic Omen
stories: In 1898 Morgan Robertson wrote 'Futility' about an unsinkable ship called the Titan that hit an iceberg and sank. 14 years later the Titanic eerily similar in size and design sank exactly the same way.

In the 1930s Joseph Figlock was walking in Detroit when a baby fell from a window and landed on him. Both survived. A year later the exact same baby fell from a window again and landed on Joseph Figlock again.
A watch recovered from a Titanic victim stopped at 2:28 AM the exact time the ship sank. In 2012 the watch sold for $28,000 exactly 100 years to the day after the sinking.

entries.title: What are the odds? Mark Twain's Comet
stories: Mark Twain was born on November 30, 1835 the same day Halley's Comet appeared. He said 'I came in with Halley's Comet and I expect to go out with it.' He died on April 21, 1910 the day after Halley's Comet returned.
In 2011 a British man was arrested in Spain. His mugshot turned out identical to a photo taken at the same police station 12 years earlier of a man who committed the exact same crime.

In 1937 an American salesman in Paris bought a postcard of the Tomb of the Unknown Soldier. He saw his own father in the crowd photo who had visited Paris 20 years earlier on a trip the son never knew about.

entries.title: The universe has a weird sense of humor: The $50 Bill
entries.hook: The universe has a weird sense of humor:
stories: In 2002 a woman in Michigan found a $50 bill and deposited it. Months later her mother gave her a birthday card with a $50 bill inside with the exact same serial number as the one she found.

In the 1930s Joseph Figlock was walking in Detroit when a baby fell from a window and landed on him. Both survived. A year later the exact same baby fell from a window again and landed on Joseph Figlock again.

In 1972 a man switched hotel rooms because of a bad feeling. His original room was destroyed in a fire. 23 years later he checked into the same hotel and was given the same room he had switched to not knowing.

entries.title: The universe has a weird sense of humor: Prison Photo Coincidence
entries.hook: The universe has a weird sense of humor:
stories: In 2011 a British man was arrested in Spain. His mugshot turned out identical to a photo taken at the same police station 12 years earlier of a man who committed the exact same crime.
In 2009 Bulgarian lottery officials drew winning numbers 4 15 23 24 35 42. Four days later the exact same numbers came up again. The odds were 1 in 4 million. No fraud was found.
A woman lost her wedding ring swimming off Sweden in 1995. Six years later a fisherman caught a fish and found the ring inside it. The inscription was still readable and he tracked her down.

entries.title: Statistically impossible yet it happened: Lincoln & Kennedy
stories: Abraham Lincoln was elected to Congress in 1846, John F. Kennedy in 1946. Lincoln was elected President in 1860, Kennedy in 1960. Both were assassinated on a Friday in front of their wives. Both successors were named Johnson.
In 1919 a molasses tank exploded in Boston sending a 15-foot wave through the streets killing 21. Exactly 50 years later in 1969 a molasses tank explosion killed 9 in Brooklyn New York.

In 1919 a molasses tank exploded in Boston sending a 15-foot wave through the streets killing 21. Exactly 50 years later in 1969 a molasses tank explosion killed 9 in Brooklyn New York. The Hotel Fire. In 1972 a man switched hotel rooms because of a bad feeling. His original room was destroyed in a fire. 23 years later he checked into the same hotel and was given the same room he had switched to not knowing.

entries.title: Statistically impossible yet it happened: Death Bed Reunion
stories: In 1883 a dying man in Detroit asked for his estranged brother. A stranger arrived hours later who had traveled 200 miles. He had received the same telegram the dying man's family sent because he had the same dying wish at the same time.

In the 1930s Joseph Figlock was walking in Detroit when a baby fell from a window and landed on him. Both survived. A year later the exact same baby fell from a window again and landed on Joseph Figlock again.

Three men in the Canary Islands discovered they were identical triplets separated at birth by an adoption agency as part of a secret study. All three grew up with similar habits and married women with the same name.

entries.title: This is 100% true: Mark Twain's Comet
stories: Mark Twain was born on November 30, 1835 the same day Halley's Comet appeared. He said 'I came in with Halley's Comet and I expect to go out with it.' He died on April 21, 1910 the day after Halley's Comet returned.

In the 1920s author Anne Parrish found a book she remembered from childhood in a Paris bookstore. She bought it and showed her husband. When she opened it she found her own name and childhood address written inside as the previous owner.

In the 1930s Joseph Figlock was walking in Detroit when a baby fell from a window and landed on him. Both survived. A year later the exact same baby fell from a window again and landed on Joseph Figlock again.

entries.title: You won't believe this coincidence: The James Twins
stories: Two twin boys in Ohio were separated at birth and adopted by different families. Both were named James. Both grew up to be police officers. Both married women named Linda. Both had sons named James Alan and James Allan. Both owned dogs named Toy.

In 1937 an American salesman in Paris bought a postcard of the Tomb of the Unknown Soldier. He saw his own father in the crowd photo who had visited Paris 20 years earlier on a trip the son never knew about.
In 2014 twin brothers in Finland were cycling separately when both were hit by cars on the same road one hour apart. Both survived. The drivers were also twins.

entries.title: What are the odds? Death Bed Reunion
stories: In 1883 a dying man in Detroit asked for his estranged brother. A stranger arrived hours later who had traveled 200 miles. He had received the same telegram the dying man's family sent because he had the same dying wish at the same time.

In the 1930s Joseph Figlock was walking in Detroit when a baby fell from a window and landed on him. Both survived. A year later the exact same baby fell from a window again and landed on Joseph Figlock again.

In 1736 composer Giuseppe Tartini dreamed the devil played a violin sonata. He wrote down 'The Devil's Trill Sonata' one of the most famous violin works. In 1959 another composer had the same dream and wrote a strikingly similar piece.

entries.title: The universe has a weird sense of humor: The Lottery Replay
entries.hook: The universe has a weird sense of humor:
stories: In 2009 Bulgarian lottery officials drew winning numbers 4 15 23 24 35 42. Four days later the exact same numbers came up again. The odds were 1 in 4 million. No fraud was found.
In 1975 two men named Robert Smith from different states sat next to each other at a Chicago convention. Both worked for the same company in different offices. Both had the same birthday.

In 1972 a man switched hotel rooms because of a bad feeling. His original room was destroyed in a fire. 23 years later he checked into the same hotel and was given the same room he had switched to not knowing.

entries.title: You won't believe this coincidence: The Lottery Replay
stories: In 2009 Bulgarian lottery officials drew winning numbers 4 15 23 24 35 42. Four days later the exact same numbers came up again. The odds were 1 in 4 million. No fraud was found.

In 1937 an American salesman in Paris bought a postcard of the Tomb of the Unknown Soldier. He saw his own father in the crowd photo who had visited Paris 20 years earlier on a trip the son never knew about.
In 1975 two men named Robert Smith from different states sat next to each other at a Chicago convention. Both worked for the same company in different offices. Both had the same birthday.

entries.title: What are the odds? Lincoln & Kennedy
stories: Abraham Lincoln was elected to Congress in 1846, John F. Kennedy in 1946. Lincoln was elected President in 1860, Kennedy in 1960. Both were assassinated on a Friday in front of their wives. Both successors were named Johnson.

In 1898 Morgan Robertson wrote 'Futility' about an unsinkable ship called the Titan that hit an iceberg and sank. 14 years later the Titanic eerily similar in size and design sank exactly the same way.
In 2008 two British women named Emily Jones booked separate trips to the same Greek resort. They had never met. They checked into the same room and took the exact same photos at the same spots.

entries.title: Statistically impossible yet it happened: Mark Twain's Comet
stories: Mark Twain was born on November 30, 1835 the same day Halley's Comet appeared. He said 'I came in with Halley's Comet and I expect to go out with it.' He died on April 21, 1910 the day after Halley's Comet returned.

In 1937 an American salesman in Paris bought a postcard of the Tomb of the Unknown Soldier. He saw his own father in the crowd photo who had visited Paris 20 years earlier on a trip the son never knew about.

Three men in the Canary Islands discovered they were identical triplets separated at birth by an adoption agency as part of a secret study. All three grew up with similar habits and married women with the same name.

entries.title: Statistically impossible yet it happened: Lincoln & Kennedy
stories: Abraham Lincoln was elected to Congress in 1846, John F. Kennedy in 1946. Lincoln was elected President in 1860, Kennedy in 1960. Both were assassinated on a Friday in front of their wives. Both successors were named Johnson.
In 2002 a woman in Michigan found a $50 bill and deposited it. Months later her mother gave her a birthday card with a $50 bill inside with the exact same serial number as the one she found.

In 1883 a dying man in Detroit asked for his estranged brother. A stranger arrived hours later who had traveled 200 miles. He had received the same telegram the dying man's family sent because he had the same dying wish at the same time.

entries.title: This is 100% true: The James Twins
stories: Two twin boys in Ohio were separated at birth and adopted by different families. Both were named James. Both grew up to be police officers. Both married women named Linda. Both had sons named James Alan and James Allan. Both owned dogs named Toy.

In the 1930s Joseph Figlock was walking in Detroit when a baby fell from a window and landed on him. Both survived. A year later the exact same baby fell from a window again and landed on Joseph Figlock again.
In 2014 twin brothers in Finland were cycling separately when both were hit by cars on the same road one hour apart. Both survived. The drivers were also twins.

entries.title: You won't believe this coincidence: The James Twins
stories: Two twin boys in Ohio were separated at birth and adopted by different families. Both were named James. Both grew up to be police officers. Both married women named Linda. Both had sons named James Alan and James Allan. Both owned dogs named Toy.

In the 1920s author Anne Parrish found a book she remembered from childhood in a Paris bookstore. She bought it and showed her husband. When she opened it she found her own name and childhood address written inside as the previous owner.
In 1919 a molasses tank exploded in Boston sending a 15-foot wave through the streets killing 21. Exactly 50 years later in 1969 a molasses tank explosion killed 9 in Brooklyn New York.

entries.title: You won't believe this coincidence: Lincoln & Kennedy
stories: Abraham Lincoln was elected to Congress in 1846, John F. Kennedy in 1946. Lincoln was elected President in 1860, Kennedy in 1960. Both were assassinated on a Friday in front of their wives. Both successors were named Johnson.
A woman lost her wedding ring swimming off Sweden in 1995. Six years later a fisherman caught a fish and found the ring inside it. The inscription was still readable and he tracked her down.

In 1736 composer Giuseppe Tartini dreamed the devil played a violin sonata. He wrote down 'The Devil's Trill Sonata' one of the most famous violin works. In 1959 another composer had the same dream and wrote a strikingly similar piece.

entries.title: Statistically impossible yet it happened: The Lottery Replay
stories: In 2009 Bulgarian lottery officials drew winning numbers 4 15 23 24 35 42. Four days later the exact same numbers came up again. The odds were 1 in 4 million. No fraud was found.

In 1883 a dying man in Detroit asked for his estranged brother. A stranger arrived hours later who had traveled 200 miles. He had received the same telegram the dying man's family sent because he had the same dying wish at the same time.

In 1736 composer Giuseppe Tartini dreamed the devil played a violin sonata. He wrote down 'The Devil's Trill Sonata' one of the most famous violin works. In 1959 another composer had the same dream and wrote a strikingly similar piece.

entries.title: Statistically impossible yet it happened: Mark Twain's Comet
stories: Mark Twain was born on November 30, 1835 the same day Halley's Comet appeared. He said 'I came in with Halley's Comet and I expect to go out with it.' He died on April 21, 1910 the day after Halley's Comet returned.

In the 1920s author Anne Parrish found a book she remembered from childhood in a Paris bookstore. She bought it and showed her husband. When she opened it she found her own name and childhood address written inside as the previous owner.

entries.title: What are the odds? Lincoln & Kennedy
stories: Abraham Lincoln was elected to Congress in 1846, John F. Kennedy in 1946. Lincoln was elected President in 1860, Kennedy in 1960. Both were assassinated on a Friday in front of their wives. Both successors were named Johnson.
In 2009 Bulgarian lottery officials drew winning numbers 4 15 23 24 35 42. Four days later the exact same numbers came up again. The odds were 1 in 4 million. No fraud was found.

In 1736 composer Giuseppe Tartini dreamed the devil played a violin sonata. He wrote down 'The Devil's Trill Sonata' one of the most famous violin works. In 1959 another composer had the same dream and wrote a strikingly similar piece.

In 2008 two British women named Emily Jones booked separate trips to the same Greek resort. They had never met. They checked into the same room and took the exact same photos at the same spots. The Unknown Soldier. In 1937 an American salesman in Paris bought a postcard of the Tomb of the Unknown Soldier. He saw his own father in the crowd photo who had visited Paris 20 years earlier on a trip the son never knew about.

He saw his own father in the crowd photo who had visited Paris 20 years earlier on a trip the son never knew about.

entries.title: What are the odds? The Titanic Omen
stories: In 1898 Morgan Robertson wrote 'Futility' about an unsinkable ship called the Titan that hit an iceberg and sank. 14 years later the Titanic eerily similar in size and design sank exactly the same way.
In 2009 Bulgarian lottery officials drew winning numbers 4 15 23 24 35 42. Four days later the exact same numbers came up again. The odds were 1 in 4 million. No fraud was found.

In 1972 a man switched hotel rooms because of a bad feeling. His original room was destroyed in a fire. 23 years later he checked into the same hotel and was given the same room he had switched to not knowing.

entries.title: You won't believe this coincidence: Mark Twain's Comet
stories: Mark Twain was born on November 30, 1835 the same day Halley's Comet appeared. He said 'I came in with Halley's Comet and I expect to go out with it.' He died on April 21, 1910 the day after Halley's Comet returned.

In 1898 Morgan Robertson wrote 'Futility' about an unsinkable ship called the Titan that hit an iceberg and sank. 14 years later the Titanic eerily similar in size and design sank exactly the same way.
In 2009 Bulgarian lottery officials drew winning numbers 4 15 23 24 35 42. Four days later the exact same numbers came up again. The odds were 1 in 4 million. No fraud was found.

entries.title: What are the odds? The James Twins
stories: Two twin boys in Ohio were separated at birth and adopted by different families. Both were named James. Both grew up to be police officers. Both married women named Linda. Both had sons named James Alan and James Allan. Both owned dogs named Toy.

Abraham Lincoln was elected to Congress in 1846, John F. Kennedy in 1946. Lincoln was elected President in 1860, Kennedy in 1960. Both were assassinated on a Friday in front of their wives. Both successors were named Johnson.

In the 1930s Joseph Figlock was walking in Detroit when a baby fell from a window and landed on him. Both survived. A year later the exact same baby fell from a window again and landed on Joseph Figlock again.

entries.title: What are the odds? Mark Twain's Comet
stories: Mark Twain was born on November 30, 1835 the same day Halley's Comet appeared. He said 'I came in with Halley's Comet and I expect to go out with it.' He died on April 21, 1910 the day after Halley's Comet returned.
A woman lost her wedding ring swimming off Sweden in 1995. Six years later a fisherman caught a fish and found the ring inside it. The inscription was still readable and he tracked her down.
In 2014 twin brothers in Finland were cycling separately when both were hit by cars on the same road one hour apart. Both survived. The drivers were also twins.

entries.title: What are the odds? The Bookstore Dream
stories: In the 1920s author Anne Parrish found a book she remembered from childhood in a Paris bookstore. She bought it and showed her husband. When she opened it she found her own name and childhood address written inside as the previous owner.
In 1919 a molasses tank exploded in Boston sending a 15-foot wave through the streets killing 21. Exactly 50 years later in 1969 a molasses tank explosion killed 9 in Brooklyn New York.

In 1736 composer Giuseppe Tartini dreamed the devil played a violin sonata. He wrote down 'The Devil's Trill Sonata' one of the most famous violin works. In 1959 another composer had the same dream and wrote a strikingly similar piece.

entries.title: You won't believe this coincidence: Death Bed Reunion
stories: In 1883 a dying man in Detroit asked for his estranged brother. A stranger arrived hours later who had traveled 200 miles. He had received the same telegram the dying man's family sent because he had the same dying wish at the same time.
In 2014 twin brothers in Finland were cycling separately when both were hit by cars on the same road one hour apart. Both survived. The drivers were also twins.

In 1972 a man switched hotel rooms because of a bad feeling. His original room was destroyed in a fire. 23 years later he checked into the same hotel and was given the same room he had switched to not knowing.

In 1883 a dying man in Detroit asked for his estranged brother. A stranger arrived hours later who had traveled 200 miles. He had received the same telegram the dying man's family sent because he had the same dying wish at the same time.

Three men in the Canary Islands discovered they were identical triplets separated at birth by an adoption agency as part of a secret study. All three grew up with similar habits and married women with the same name.

entries.title: You won't believe this coincidence: Death Bed Reunion
stories: In 1883 a dying man in Detroit asked for his estranged brother. A stranger arrived hours later who had traveled 200 miles. He had received the same telegram the dying man's family sent because he had the same dying wish at the same time.

In the 1930s Joseph Figlock was walking in Detroit when a baby fell from a window and landed on him. Both survived. A year later the exact same baby fell from a window again and landed on Joseph Figlock again.

In 1937 an American salesman in Paris bought a postcard of the Tomb of the Unknown Soldier. He saw his own father in the crowd photo who had visited Paris 20 years earlier on a trip the son never knew about.

entries.title: Statistically impossible yet it happened: The Bookstore Dream
stories: In the 1920s author Anne Parrish found a book she remembered from childhood in a Paris bookstore. She bought it and showed her husband. When she opened it she found her own name and childhood address written inside as the previous owner.
In 1919 a molasses tank exploded in Boston sending a 15-foot wave through the streets killing 21. Exactly 50 years later in 1969 a molasses tank explosion killed 9 in Brooklyn New York.

In 1937 an American salesman in Paris bought a postcard of the Tomb of the Unknown Soldier. He saw his own father in the crowd photo who had visited Paris 20 years earlier on a trip the son never knew about.

entries.title: What are the odds? The $50 Bill
stories: In 2002 a woman in Michigan found a $50 bill and deposited it. Months later her mother gave her a birthday card with a $50 bill inside with the exact same serial number as the one she found.
In 2009 Bulgarian lottery officials drew winning numbers 4 15 23 24 35 42. Four days later the exact same numbers came up again. The odds were 1 in 4 million. No fraud was found.

In 1972 a man switched hotel rooms because of a bad feeling. His original room was destroyed in a fire. 23 years later he checked into the same hotel and was given the same room he had switched to not knowing.

entries.title: You won't believe this coincidence: Lincoln & Kennedy

In the 1920s author Anne Parrish found a book she remembered from childhood in a Paris bookstore. She bought it and showed her husband. When she opened it she found her own name and childhood address written inside as the previous owner.
In 2014 twin brothers in Finland were cycling separately when both were hit by cars on the same road one hour apart. Both survived. The drivers were also twins.

entries.title: Statistically impossible yet it happened: Mark Twain's Comet
stories: Mark Twain was born on November 30, 1835 the same day Halley's Comet appeared. He said 'I came in with Halley's Comet and I expect to go out with it.' He died on April 21, 1910 the day after Halley's Comet returned.
A woman lost her wedding ring swimming off Sweden in 1995. Six years later a fisherman caught a fish and found the ring inside it. The inscription was still readable and he tracked her down.

In 1972 a man switched hotel rooms because of a bad feeling. His original room was destroyed in a fire. 23 years later he checked into the same hotel and was given the same room he had switched to not knowing.

Months later her mother gave her a birthday card with a $50 bill inside with the exact same serial number as the one she found. The Unknown Soldier. In 1937 an American salesman in Paris bought a postcard of the Tomb of the Unknown Soldier. He saw his own father in the crowd photo who had visited Paris 20 years earlier on a trip the son never knew about.

In 1937 an American salesman in Paris bought a postcard of the Tomb of the Unknown Soldier. entries.title: What are the odds? The Titanic Omen
stories: In 1898 Morgan Robertson wrote 'Futility' about an unsinkable ship called the Titan that hit an iceberg and sank. 14 years later the Titanic eerily similar in size and design sank exactly the same way.
In 2009 Bulgarian lottery officials drew winning numbers 4 15 23 24 35 42. Four days later the exact same numbers came up again. The odds were 1 in 4 million. No fraud was found.
In 2014 twin brothers in Finland were cycling separately when both were hit by cars on the same road one hour apart. Both survived. The drivers were also twins.

entries.title: The universe has a weird sense of humor: The Bookstore Dream
entries.hook: The universe has a weird sense of humor:
stories: In the 1920s author Anne Parrish found a book she remembered from childhood in a Paris bookstore. She bought it and showed her husband. When she opened it she found her own name and childhood address written inside as the previous owner.

In 1883 a dying man in Detroit asked for his estranged brother. A stranger arrived hours later who had traveled 200 miles. He had received the same telegram the dying man's family sent because he had the same dying wish at the same time.

In the 1930s Joseph Figlock was walking in Detroit when a baby fell from a window and landed on him. Both survived. A year later the exact same baby fell from a window again and landed on Joseph Figlock again.

entries.title: Statistically impossible yet it happened: The Lost Ring
stories: A woman lost her wedding ring swimming off Sweden in 1995. Six years later a fisherman caught a fish and found the ring inside it. The inscription was still readable and he tracked her down.

In 1972 a man switched hotel rooms because of a bad feeling. His original room was destroyed in a fire. 23 years later he checked into the same hotel and was given the same room he had switched to not knowing.
A watch recovered from a Titanic victim stopped at 2:28 AM the exact time the ship sank. In 2012 the watch sold for $28,000 exactly 100 years to the day after the sinking.

entries.title: What are the odds? Mark Twain's Comet
stories: Mark Twain was born on November 30, 1835 the same day Halley's Comet appeared. He said 'I came in with Halley's Comet and I expect to go out with it.' He died on April 21, 1910 the day after Halley's Comet returned.

In 1736 composer Giuseppe Tartini dreamed the devil played a violin sonata. He wrote down 'The Devil's Trill Sonata' one of the most famous violin works. In 1959 another composer had the same dream and wrote a strikingly similar piece.

Three men in the Canary Islands discovered they were identical triplets separated at birth by an adoption agency as part of a secret study. All three grew up with similar habits and married women with the same name.
